mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate reduces discount rates for equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending recession, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad consumer demand strength, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal yields negative 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production peaks during late-cycle expansions indicate capacity constraints and rising input costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle phases, or Rank IC drops below 0.02 in regime-specific walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index values above 225 trigger inflation fears and anticipated central bank tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of high-inflation trigger instances, or predictive power vanishes after controlling for rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls reflect robust economic activity and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows, or payroll growth shows no significant correlation with 60-day equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: High real GDP growth combined with elevated unemployment indicates stagflationary dynamics, suppressing corporate margins and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect yields positive returns in > 50% of cases, or joint predictor loses out-of-sample explanatory power compared to univariate models.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production levels signal strong manufacturing output and supply chain efficiency, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of high-production observations, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested windows.
